rationale: The benchmark ceiling (DoS through input validation bypass) demonstrates basic code generation and vulnerability exploitation, which transfers weakly to Execution phase tradecraft. An LLM at this capability level can assist OC3 actors by accelerating script generation, providing obfuscation patterns, and suggesting LOLBAS variations, but cannot fundamentally improve operational security or EDR evasion. This justifies a modest 6-11 percentage point uplift from the 50% baseline, reflecting faster iteration rather than new capabilities.
